name: Artichoke Baskets with Caper Dip
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the artichokes: With a sharp knife, cut off the stems. Using scissors, remove the prickly tips. Rinse and shake off excess water. Wrap the artichokes in plastic wrap and place them on a microwave-safe plate or in a microwave-safe casserole dish. Add 1/4 cup water and cover. Place in the microwave and cook on high for 5-8 minutes, or until the lower leaves can be easily pulled off and pierced with a fork. Rotate and rearrange halfway through cooking. Let stand 3-5 minutes.
2. Prepare the dip: In a small bowl, combine the fat-free mayonnaise, minced garlic, capers (or pickle), and caper brine.
3. Prepare the baskets: Remove about 1/4 of one side of the artichoke leaves and reserve them. Carefully remove and discard the choke. Place on a plate. Fill the inside of the basket with the caper dip and allow some to spill out onto the plate. Chill until ready to serve. If desired, serve with the reserved artichoke leaves.
4. Note: The artichokes can also be cooked on the stove top by placing the trimmed artichokes stem-side down in a small saucepot that is large enough to hold them snugly. Pour boiling water into the pot until it reaches a depth of 1 inch, cover, and steam for 45 minutes over medium heat, or until tender. Remove with tongs, drain, and serve. (Makes 2 servings, 129 calories each.)
